title: carpogymnia dryopteris var. pumila
intro: Carpogymnia dryopteris var. pumila, also known as Dwarf Wood Fern, is a rare and small variety of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carpogymnia dryopteris var. pumila include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and improving digestion, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
